seoTitle: How to Add an Accountant to QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Grant your accountant access to QuickBooks Online so they can handle bookkeeping, tax prep, and financial analysis without sharing your login.
intro: Giving your accountant direct access to QuickBooks Online makes collaboration on bookkeeping, tax preparation, and financial analysis much smoother than exchanging spreadsheets. This guide covers granting accountant access properly and managing permissions.

steps.heading: Open the manage users settings
steps.body: Go to the area in QuickBooks Online where team and accountant access is managed.

steps.heading: Invite your accountant by email
steps.body: Enter your accountant's email address to send them an invitation to access your company file.

steps.heading: Set the appropriate access level
steps.body: Confirm the accountant role is applied so they receive the right level of access for handling your books and reports.

steps.heading: Have your accountant accept the invite
steps.body: Your accountant will need to accept the invitation from their end before access is fully active.

steps.heading: Review and manage access over time
steps.body: Periodically check who has accountant access and remove it if you change accounting firms or no longer need the connection.

faq.question: Does adding an accountant give them full access to my account?
faq.answer: Accountant access is designed for handling financial data and reports, and QuickBooks lets you review what level of access has been granted.

faq.question: Can I remove an accountant's access later?
faq.answer: Yes, you can revoke accountant access at any time through the same user management settings used to grant it.

faq.question: Is there a cost to adding an accountant to my QuickBooks account?
faq.answer: Accountant access is typically included as part of your existing QuickBooks Online subscription rather than requiring an extra fee.

faq.question: What if my accountant doesn't receive the invitation email?
faq.answer: Double-check the email address entered and ask them to check their spam folder, or resend the invitation from your user management settings.
